task_completion.description: Log in to Oscar EMR using the provided credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Oscar EMR main dashboard or schedule screen is visible, indicating a successful login (typically showing 'oscardoc' as the logged-in user).

task_completion.description: Locate the high-priority tickler for patient Marcus Aurelius regarding the critical lab result.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A tickler list or patient chart is visible showing a tickler for 'Marcus Aurelius' with a message containing 'Critical Lab Result: Potassium 6.2'.

task_completion.description: Open the specific tickler to process and update it.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An edit or update screen/modal specifically for the 'Potassium 6.2' tickler is open and active.

task_completion.description: Update the tickler with the exact required note.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Spoke to patient at 09:00. Advised to go to ER immediately. Patient confirmed they are leaving now.' is typed into the tickler's note or message text area.

task_completion.description: Mark the tickler as 'Complete' and save.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The status dropdown or radio button for the tickler is set to 'Complete' (or similar completed status), OR the final screenshot shows the active tickler list with this specific tickler no longer present/moved to a completed view.

integrity.description: The agent used the Oscar EMR web interface rather than terminal commands or API calls.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the Oscar EMR UI, rather than a terminal window executing curl commands or database scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Oscar EMR application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text entry and status updates occur within the Oscar EMR interface (identifiable by its standard medical record UI elements), not in a generic text editor or a mocked HTML page.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the UI to update the database directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows visible showing MySQL/MariaDB queries updating the tickler tables directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Oscar EMR is a web-based Electronic Medical Record system. The target patient is 'Marcus Aurelius'. The tickler message to look for contains 'Critical Lab Result: Potassium 6.2'. The exact note that must be entered is: 'Spoke to patient at 09:00. Advised to go to ER immediately. Patient confirmed they are leaving now.' The task requires changing the tickler status to 'Complete' via the UI.
